why_them: Website is a basic Wix build with virtually no content beyond contact details — no class schedule, no personal training packages, no online booking or payment capability. Despite being named Best Personal Trainer in Dumfries & Galloway 2023, their digital presence completely undersells the business. A professional site with service pages, booking, and testimonials would directly convert the premium reputation they've already earned.
recommended_service: New WordPress or Squarespace site with personal training package pages, lead capture form, results gallery, and contact/booking integration

why_them: 25+ years of experience, multiple class types (adults kickboxing, children's, women's self-defence, private training) but no online booking, no class schedule on the website, and no payment integration. Also has a second abandoned UENI placeholder site showing they've tried and failed to get a proper online presence. A new site with timetable, trial class booking, and a structured children's programme page would immediately lift enquiries.
recommended_service: New site with class timetable, online trial class booking, children's programme page, instructor bio, and contact form

location: Caledonian Place, Lockerbie, DG11 2EX / High Street, Annan, DG12 6EJ
why_them: A fast-growing independent gym that has just expanded from Lockerbie to a second Annan site in 2025. Despite this growth, the website has no online booking, no class schedule, and no membership sign-up flow. Two-location gym needing to consolidate its digital presence and drive sign-ups across both sites from a single professional platform.
recommended_service: New multi-location website with online membership purchase, class timetables for both sites, and a mobile-optimised gym tour with opening hours

location: St Marys Industrial Estate, Dumfries, DG1 1NA (plus Dalbeattie, Lockerbie, Lochmaben, Moffat)
why_them: Five-venue operation across D&G with reformer pilates, Barre, Bowen Technique, and Bungeefit but zero online booking — all bookings by phone/text/email only. With reformer sessions commanding £20-£30/class, this is a high-value business losing revenue to friction in the booking process. A modern site with online scheduling and pre-payment would dramatically reduce admin and no-shows.
recommended_service: Booking system integration (e.g. Acuity or Calendly Pro embedded), class timetable redesign showing all 5 venues, and online gift vouchers

why_them: A 24/7 gym with a genuinely terrible website — just a 'Newly Renovated' banner, contact details, and almost no other content. No membership sign-up, no pricing shown online, no class or PT info. Memberships start from £15/month suggesting real commercial volume, but the website drives zero conversions. Freshly renovated premises signals the owner is investing in the facility.
recommended_service: New site with membership sign-up flow, pricing page, gym tour/photos, class timetable, and online enquiry form

why_them: D&G's self-styled 'premier' dance school, one of the first dedicated dance studios in Dumfries (opened 2007), operating for 17+ years — but entirely Facebook-dependent with no standalone website. A long-established school of this standing losing ground digitally while newer rivals have professional multi-feature sites is a compelling pitch.
recommended_service: New website with class timetable, online enrolment, gallery of performances, news/events, and Google Maps/SEO

why_them: Facebook-only — no website at all. An established, community-rooted boxing club affiliated with Boxing Scotland, with competitive success highlighted in recent Boxing Scotland videos. Completely invisible to anyone outside Facebook. A simple but professional site with junior/adult class info, training schedule, membership fees, and club history would be transformative.
recommended_service: Starter website: home, classes/schedule, join us page, results/news page, contact — mobile-first

location: The Old School, Brooke Street, Dumfries
why_them: Website throws SSL certificate errors meaning visitors get browser security warnings before they even see the site — a critical trust-killer for parents signing up children. Facebook-primary in practice. A new site on reliable hosting with SSL, class timetable, and parent registration flow would pay for itself quickly.
recommended_service: New website with SSL, class timetable, parent info/FAQ, photo gallery, online enquiry/registration form

location: The Shed, Stewartry Rugby Club, Greenlaw, Castle Douglas, DG7 2LH
why_them: A functional fitness and CrossFit-style gym in Castle Douglas with a timetable page but no online class booking or payment capability — customers must email a Gmail address to join. Offers specialist Teens and Legends (60+) classes which are highly marketable segments. A modern booking site would help them compete with urban CrossFit boxes.
recommended_service: New site with online class booking and membership management, dedicated programme pages for Teens and Legends classes, and a free trial call-to-action

why_them: The domain crossfitcastledouglas.com is completely dead (DNS not resolving) despite the gym actively operating and maintaining a Facebook page. CrossFit affiliates nationally command premium memberships (£80-£150/month), yet this one has no web presence at all. A clean affiliate-style site with intro classes, pricing, and WOD blog would bring it in line with affiliate standards.
recommended_service: New CrossFit affiliate website: intro programme page, membership pricing, class schedule, coaches page, WOD archive, and contact/booking

why_them: Small-town 24/7 gym with a mid-range website listing gymnastics, court hire, classes, and personal training but with no online booking or payment flow. Moffat is a tourist town on the A74 with visitor footfall — missing an opportunity to convert passing tourist traffic with a clear 'day pass' or 'visitor membership' digital CTA.
recommended_service: Website upgrade with online class booking, visitor/day pass purchase, and improved mobile UX — add a gymnastics programme landing page

why_them: Has a class schedule and equipment directory on the site, but no visible online booking or payment system and no membership purchase flow. A registered limited company suggesting commercial intent and scale. The gap between good branding and missing conversion tools is the opportunity.
recommended_service: Add online membership sign-up and payment integration, direct debit/recurring billing, and online class booking to existing site

why_them: Wix-built site for a RAD Ballet, Jazz, Musical Theatre and Acro Dance school. Uses ClassForKids for bookings (a third-party widget) but the main website is generic Wix with limited personalised content. No SEO-optimised service pages limiting discoverability. A properly built site integrating ClassForKids with strong visual content and SEO could significantly grow their pupil base.
recommended_service: New custom-built site replacing Wix: dedicated programme pages, ClassForKids integration, photo/video gallery, SEO for local dance school searches

why_them: Listed on multiple directories with a Gmail address and Facebook presence only — no website. The club recently relocated from Dumfries to Lochmaben, meaning anyone Googling the old address finds only outdated directory listings. A new website would simultaneously fix this critical visibility problem, announce the new venue, and establish proper class booking.
recommended_service: New website: venue details, class timetable, beginner's guide page, contact/enquiry form, and Google Business Profile alignment

why_them: A newly renovated facility with glass-back courts, biomass boiler, cafe bar, and pro shop — genuinely premium physical product. But the website is basic HTML, primarily informational, despite mentioning an 'online court booking system'. The disconnect between the quality of the physical club and the quality of the digital presence is stark.
recommended_service: Modern membership website with integrated court booking, online membership join/payment, league fixtures, and pro shop e-commerce

why_them: Multi-sport club (tennis, squash, cricket, hockey, running, gym) with a basic WordPress site. Court booking links out to a separate third-party system, membership info is basic, and there is no unified digital hub for all the different sports sections. A club of this scale and multi-sport range is under-served digitally.
recommended_service: Full website redesign with integrated court booking, online membership sign-up and renewal, separate sport-section pages, and club news/events calendar

why_them: A historic 1903 championship-grade links course with online booking already in place, but the design is traditional/dated, membership applications are PDF-based rather than digital forms, and the site lacks modern SEO and content marketing. As a premium golf venue on the Solway coast, it should be ranking better for 'Solway golf' and 'Dumfries golf breaks'.
recommended_service: Website modernisation: updated design, digital membership application form, SEO content strategy for golf tourism, integrated tee booking

why_them: Professional credentials (I-PATH, FLexercise, Popdance Partner, CDMT affiliations) but the members area and fee payment system appear to be a legacy custom-built solution. No general online class booking for new prospects — trial class booking exists but the journey is clunky. An 18-year-old school positioning for the next chapter would benefit from streamlined new-pupil onboarding.
recommended_service: Website redesign and modernisation: new-pupil journey, unified booking and payment platform, updated branding and mobile UX

why_them: National Geographic Cool List 2024 winner, Scottish Thistle Award Regional winner, 100+ five-star reviews — an exceptional product. Website is Squarespace and reasonably professional, but tour booking and payment flows could be better optimised and the site lacks a blog/content strategy to capture organic search from 'Galloway cycling holidays' terms. Lower need score but the budget is clearly there.
recommended_service: Conversion rate optimisation, integrated booking with upfront deposit/payment, blog/content strategy for cycling tourism SEO
